name: Google Gemini 2.0 Flash
pricing_notes: Gemini 2.0 Flash: $0.075/M input tokens (<128K), $0.30/M output; free tier: 15 RPM

auth.notes: GOOGLE_API_KEY; Vertex AI authentication for enterprise

performance.notes: Fastest Gemini model; designed for real-time applications; multimodal input with low added latency

agent_readiness.notes: 1M context window. Native multimodal (text/image/video/audio). Function calling. Code execution. Grounding with Google Search. Thinking mode (experimental). Fast and cheap — best value in Gemini family.

gotchas: Free tier (15 RPM) too restrictive for development beyond experiments
Context caching required for cost efficiency on repeated long-context calls
Gemini 1.5 Pro still outperforms Flash on complex reasoning tasks
Native libraries less mature than OpenAI SDK ecosystem
Function calling response format differs from OpenAI (migration effort)
best_when: High-throughput, cost-sensitive LLM inference needing multimodal input
Agents processing mixed media inputs (text + images + documents)
avoid_when: Need maximum reasoning accuracy (use Gemini 2.0 Pro or Claude Opus)
Production without Vertex AI (AI Studio lacks enterprise SLAs)
summary: Gemini 2.0 Flash is Google's fastest and most cost-efficient frontier model with 1M context window, native multimodal support, and function calling. Excellent price-performance ratio ($0.075/M input) for high-throughput applications. Native image/video/audio input is a genuine differentiator. Best for real-time or bulk workloads where Gemini Pro's quality isn't necessary.
